metadata.purpose: Assess prerequisite knowledge before learning to round 4-digit numbers

prerequisiteConcepts.conceptName: Number line and position of numbers

questions.question: What is the place value of 7 in the number 3,725?
questions.explanation: The digit 7 is in the hundreds place. So its place value is 7 × 100 = 700.

commonMistakes.mistake: Confused face value (7) with place value (700)
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding difference between face value and place value

commonMistakes.mistake: Thought 7 is in tens place instead of hundreds
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Counting places from right to left

questions.question: Which digit is in the tens place in the number 6,842?
questions.explanation: Reading from right to left: 2 is in ones place, 4 is in tens place, 8 is in hundreds place, 6 is in thousands place.

questions.question: True or False: In the number 5,349, the digit 3 is in the hundreds place.
questions.explanation: True! Counting from right: 9 (ones), 4 (tens), 3 (hundreds), 5 (thousands). The digit 3 is in the hundreds place.

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice identifying place positions from right to left

questions.question: Round 47 to the nearest ten.
questions.explanation: 47 is between 40 and 50. The ones digit is 7, which is 5 or above. So we round up to 50.

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Rounding rule: 5 and above rounds up

commonMistakes.mistake: Chose the midpoint instead of rounding

questions.question: Round 234 to the nearest hundred.
questions.explanation: 234 is between 200 and 300. The tens digit is 3, which is below 5. So we round down to 200.

commonMistakes.remediation: Review rounding rules and when to round down

commonMistakes.mistake: Rounded to nearest ten instead of hundred
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Reading question carefully - what to round to
commonMistakes.remediation: Pay attention to 'round to nearest...' instruction

questions.question: Round 685 to the nearest ten.
questions.explanation: 685 is between 680 and 690. The ones digit is 5, which rounds up. So 685 rounds to 690.

commonMistakes.mistake: Rounded to nearest hundred instead of ten
commonMistakes.remediation: Pay attention to what place to round to

questions.question: On a number line, which number is closer to 50: 46 or 54?
questions.explanation: 46 is 4 units away from 50 (50 - 46 = 4). 54 is 4 units away from 50 (54 - 50 = 4). Both numbers are exactly 4 units from 50, so they are equally close.

commonMistakes.mistake: Picked the smaller number without calculating distance
Picked the larger number without calculating distance

questions.question: True or False: On a number line, 38 is closer to 40 than to 30.
questions.explanation: True! 38 is 2 units from 40 (40 - 38 = 2) and 8 units from 30 (38 - 30 = 8). So 38 is closer to 40.

commonMistakes.remediation: Practice finding which number is closer

questions.question: When rounding, if the digit you're looking at is 5 or greater, you should:
questions.explanation: The rounding rule states: if the digit is 5 or above (5, 6, 7, 8, 9), round up. If it's below 5 (0, 1, 2, 3, 4), round down.

commonMistakes.remediation: Memorize: 5 or above, round up; below 5, round down

questions.question: Which of the following statements about rounding is TRUE?
options: Rounding always makes a number larger
Rounding always makes a number smaller
Rounding changes a number to the nearest specified place value
questions.correctAnswerText: Rounding changes a number to the nearest specified place value
questions.explanation: Rounding means changing a number to the nearest ten, hundred, thousand, etc. Sometimes this makes the number larger (rounding up) and sometimes smaller (rounding down).

commonMistakes.conceptGap: Understanding that rounding can go up or down
commonMistakes.remediation: See examples of rounding both up and down

commonMistakes.mistake: Didn't understand rounding works with all numbers
commonMistakes.conceptGap: Rounding is applicable to any number
commonMistakes.remediation: See rounding examples with different sized numbers

excellent.message: Excellent! You have strong prerequisite knowledge of place value and rounding. You're ready to learn rounding 4-digit numbers.

good.message: Good work! You understand the basics of rounding. Let's build on that with 4-digit numbers.

partial.message: You have some gaps in prerequisite knowledge. Watch review videos on place value and basic rounding before learning the new content.

needs_review.message: You need to review prerequisite concepts first. We'll start with place value and basic rounding, then you can learn about rounding 4-digit numbers.

feedbackMessages.on_completion: Great job completing the readiness assessment!
feedbackMessages.low_score_encouragement: Don't worry! We'll show you videos to help you review place value and rounding concepts.
feedbackMessages.high_score_praise: Fantastic! You're well-prepared to learn about rounding 4-digit numbers.
feedbackMessages.retake_option: After watching the recommended videos, you can retake this assessment to check your progress.
